perturbation.original_text: 2.7 Within ten (10) business days of receipt of FormFactor's written request, Recipient will return to <PAGE> FormFactor all information and materials, including but not limited to documents, drawings, programs, lists, models, records, compilations, notes, extracts, summaries, and any samples or parts containing Confidential Information, and all copies thereof containing Confidential Information, regardless of whether prepared by FormFactor or Recipient or any of its subsidiaries, affiliates or representatives. Fur purposes of this Paragraph 2.7, the term "documents" includes all information fixed in any tangible medium or expression, in whatever furor or format whether known or hereafter created.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.7 Within ten (10) business days of receipt of FormFactor's written request, Recipient will return to <PAGE> FormFactor all information and materials, including but not limited to documents, drawings, programs, lists, models, records, compilations, notes, extracts, summaries, and any samples or parts containing Confidential Information, and all copies thereof containing Confidential Information, regardless of whether prepared by FormFactor or Recipient or any of its subsidiaries, affiliates or representatives.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the sentence 'Fur purposes of this Paragraph 2.7, the term "documents" includes all information fixed in any tangible medium or expression, in whatever furor or format whether known or hereafter created,' from Section 2.7, the definition of 'documents' becomes unclear. The paragraph refers to returning 'documents' containing Confidential Information, but the definition of documents is left ambiguous. Without this clarification, there is now an in-text contradiction related to what documents must be returned, creating enforcement issues. Some documents may not be in a tangible medium or expression and not be covered as document.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.7 Within ten (10) business days of receipt of FormFactor's written request, Recipient will return to <PAGE> FormFactor all information and materials, including but not limited to documents, drawings, programs, lists, models, records, compilations, notes, extracts, summaries, and any samples or parts containing Confidential Information, and all copies thereof containing Confidential Information, regardless of whether prepared by FormFactor or Recipient or any of its subsidiaries, affiliates or representatives.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly defines 'documents' in Section 2.7. The changed text omits this definition. The contradicted text is the same Section 2.7, which now lacks a clear definition of 'documents'. This omission creates a contradiction because it introduces ambiguity regarding what constitutes a 'document' that must be returned, directly impacting the enforceability and interpretation of the obligation within the same section of the contract. This aligns with the Omissions - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: 3. EXPORT RESTRICTIONS. Recipient agrees that it will not in any form export, reexport, resell, ship or divert or cause to be exported, reexported, resold, stripped or diverted, directly or indirectly, any product or technical data to any country for which the United States Government or any agency thereof at the time of export or reexport requires an export license or other government approval without first obtaining such approval.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. EXPORT RESTRICTIONS. Recipient agrees that it will not in any form export, reexport, resell, ship or divert or cause to be exported, reexported, resold, stripped or diverted, directly or indirectly, any product or technical data to any country.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'for which the United States Government or any agency thereof at the time of export or reexport requires an export license or other government approval without first obtaining such approval' from Section 3, the export restriction becomes absolute. While seemingly strengthening the clause, it creates an in-text contradiction. The recipient would be unable to ship to any other country without violating the contract. Such a restriction is highly unlikely to be agreed upon and contradicts the Purpose clause that discusses a 'mutual interest in a business relationship' - that would surely entail working with other countries.
perturbation.contradicted_text: FormFactor desires to disclose, and Recipient desires to receive for its own internal evaluation, information relating to certain of FormFactor's technologies and business strategies, which information is deemed to be confidential, secret and/or proprietary to FormFactor, for the sole purpose of assisting in the determination of their mutual interest in a business relationship ("Purpose").
perturbation.justification: The original text limited export restrictions to countries requiring US government approval. The changed text removes this limitation, creating a complete export ban. This directly contradicts the 'Purpose' clause, which outlines a 'mutual interest in a business relationship' that inherently involves international collaboration. The altered Section 3, now preventing all exports, directly contradicts the Purpose, thus creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 4. TERMS. This Agreement shall be effective as of the Effective Date and may be terminated by FormFactor with respect to further disclosures upon thirty (30) days written notice. All obligations of confidentiality and restrictions on the use of Confidential Information created under and by this Agreement shall remain in force and effect for five (5) years from the date any Confidential Information is or was disclosed by FormFactor Recipient or, in the event that FormFactor and the Recipient enter into a business relationship following the date of this Agreement, five (5) years following the date such business relationship terminates, whichever is later. All other terms and conditions of this Agreement shall survive the termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. TERMS. This Agreement shall be effective as of the Effective Date and may be terminated by FormFactor with respect to further disclosures upon thirty (30) days written notice. All obligations of confidentiality and restrictions on the use of Confidential Information created under and by this Agreement shall remain in force and effect for five (5) years from the date any Confidential Information is or was disclosed by FormFactor Recipient or, in the event that FormFactor and the Recipient enter into a business relationship following the date of this Agreement, five (5) years following the date such business relationship terminates, whichever is later.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'All other terms and conditions of this Agreement shall survive the termination of this Agreement' from Section 4, it is not clear whether or not the term survive if the confidential information is exposed or disclosed, this omission is in contradiction to the term of confidentiality outlined in the original text and leaves a space for interpretation that is inconsistent with the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. TERMS. This Agreement shall be effective as of the Effective Date and may be terminated by FormFactor with respect to further disclosures upon thirty (30) days written notice. All obligations of confidentiality and restrictions on the use of Confidential Information created under and by this Agreement shall remain in force and effect for five (5) years from the date any Confidential Information is or was disclosed by FormFactor Recipient or, in the event that FormFactor and the Recipient enter into a business relationship following the date of this Agreement, five (5) years following the date such business relationship terminates, whichever is later.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that 'All other terms and conditions of this Agreement shall survive the termination of this Agreement.' The changed text omits this sentence. This omission creates a contradiction because without this survival clause, it becomes unclear whether the confidentiality obligations, which are intended to last for five years after termination of a business relationship, remain in effect after the agreement itself is terminated. The contradicted text which is also the original text contains the survival clause. Therefore, the removal of this clause in the changed text directly contradicts the intent expressed in the contradicted text regarding the ongoing enforceability of the confidentiality obligations beyond the agreement's termination, fitting the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: 2.8 Recipient hereby acknowledges and agrees that unauthorized use or disclosure of Confidential Information Would cause serious and irreparable harm and significant injury to FormFactor that may be difficult or impossible to ascertain. Accordingly, Recipient agrees that FormFactor will have, in addition to all outer remedies at law or in equity, the right to seek and obtain immediate injunctive relief for the actual or threatened unauthorized use or disclosure of Confidential Information. Recipient shall notify FormFactor immediately upon the discovery of any unauthorized disclosure or use of Confidential Information, or any other breach of this Agreement by Recipient. Recipient will cooperate with FormFactor in every reasonable way to help FormFactor regain possession of the Confidential Information and prevent further unauthorized use.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.8 Recipient hereby acknowledges and agrees that unauthorized use or disclosure of Confidential Information Would cause serious and irreparable harm and significant injury to FormFactor that may be difficult or impossible to ascertain. Recipient shall notify FormFactor immediately upon the discovery of any unauthorized disclosure or use of Confidential Information, or any other breach of this Agreement by Recipient. Recipient will cooperate with FormFactor in every reasonable way to help FormFactor regain possession of the Confidential Information and prevent further unauthorized use.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'Accordingly, Recipient agrees that FormFactor will have, in addition to all outer remedies at law or in equity, the right to seek and obtain immediate injunctive relief for the actual or threatened unauthorized use or disclosure of Confidential Information.' from Section 2.8, FormFactor's ability to seek injunctive relief is removed. While the section begins by stating that unauthorized disclosure would cause irreparable harm, removing the explicit right to injunctive relief creates a contradiction. It acknowledges the harm but then seemingly restricts the primary remedy for that harm. This is an in-text contradiction relating to remedies, creating ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.8 Recipient hereby acknowledges and agrees that unauthorized use or disclosure of Confidential Information Would cause serious and irreparable harm and significant injury to FormFactor that may be difficult or impossible to ascertain. Recipient shall notify FormFactor immediately upon the discovery of any unauthorized disclosure or use of Confidential Information, or any other breach of this Agreement by Recipient. Recipient will cooperate with FormFactor in every reasonable way to help FormFactor regain possession of the Confidential Information and prevent further unauthorized use.
perturbation.justification: The original text grants FormFactor the right to seek injunctive relief. The changed text removes this right, while the contradicted text, which is also the original text, states that unauthorized use of confidential information would cause irreparable harm. Removing the explicit right to injunctive relief contradicts the acknowledgment of irreparable harm, because injunctive relief would be the primary remedy for such harm. This leads to a strong in-text contradiction because it acknowledges the harm but removes a crucial remedy.
